title: Aidia oxyodonta
taxonomy: Aidia oxyodonta belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. Aidia oxyodonta is native to tropical regions, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. The classification of Aidia oxyodonta is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Aidia, Species: A. oxyodonta. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Aidia oxyodonta, also known as the "wild coffee tree," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, coffee-like berries. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aidia oxyodonta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aidia oxyodonta is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health complaints.
